question_text: A student builds a circuit with a 6 V battery and measures the voltage across one resistor as 2 V. In a series circuit, what would be the voltage across a second identical resistor, and why?
correct_answer: 2 V. In a series circuit with identical resistors, the voltage divides equally between them, so each identical resistor has the same voltage drop.
explanation: In a series circuit, voltage is shared between components based on their resistance. Since identical resistors have equal resistance, they share the voltage equally. If one has 2 V, the other must also have 2 V (total 4 V for two resistors, leaving 2 V for other components or if there are only two, this accounts for the full 6 V minus any other resistances).
